verdict: MIXED — 2/5 claims pass, 3/5 fail. Typecheck has pre-existing errors plus one lane-induced type error.

lane_relevant_errors.message: Argument of type 'Promise<T>' is not assignable to parameter of type 'Promise<void>'.
lane_relevant_errors.root_cause: writeQueues is Map<string, Promise<void>> but serializedWrite returns Promise<T>

lane_relevant_errors.message: Property 'installCli' does not exist on type 'ElectronAPI'.

claim_atoms.description: All 6 dedicated store IPC handlers wrapped in try/catch with console.error

details.summary: 3/6 handlers wrapped (get/set-mcp-servers, set-plugin-config). 3/6 unwrapped: get/set/delete-desktop-custom-agents and get-desktop-plugin-config.

claim_atoms.description: All window.api calls in dialog-*.tsx use optional chaining ?.()

details.summary: Only 5/9 window.api calls use ?.(). The getDesktopPluginConfig call in dialog-install-plugin.tsx and all three calls in the main component body (loadConfigs, toggleEnabled, removePlugin) lack optional chaining.

claim_atoms.description: toggleEnabled, removePlugin, submitInstall fetch fresh state from store instead of local signal

toggleEnabled.behavior: Uses configs() local signal to compute update, sends to store, uses returned result. Only fetches fresh state on catch via loadConfigs().

removePlugin.behavior: Uses configs() local signal to compute update, sends to store. Only fetches fresh state on catch via loadConfigs().

submitInstall.behavior: Calls window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig?.() to get fresh state before computing update.

details.summary: Only submitInstall fetches fresh state. toggleEnabled and removePlugin still operate on stale local signal.

claim_atoms.description: Dynamic import in index.tsx has .catch() handler

details.evidence: void import('./dialog-manage-plugins').then((x) => { ... }).catch((e) => { console.error('Failed to load plugin manager dialog:', e) })
details.catch_handler: console.error('Failed to load plugin manager dialog:', e)

details.evidence: catch (e) { console.warn('Failed to load plugin configs:', e); setConfigs([]); setDropped(0); }
details.catch_log: console.warn('Failed to load plugin configs:', e)

findings.summary: 3/6 dedicated store IPC handlers lack try/catch guards. Custom-agents handlers (get, set, delete) and get-desktop-plugin-config handler are unprotected against store read/write failures.

Only 5/9 window.api calls across both dialog files use optional chaining. The getDesktopPluginConfig read call in dialog-install-plugin.tsx and all three component-body calls (loadConfigs, toggleEnabled, removePlugin) in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx are unprotected.

toggleEnabled and removePlugin compute updates from the local configs() signal instead of fetching fresh state from the store. This creates a race condition: if another dialog or process modifies plugin config between mount and action, the stale update overwrites those changes.

ipc.ts line 71 has a type error (TS2345) caused by serializedWrite returning Promise<T> but writeQueues being typed as Map<string, Promise<void>>. Pre-existing, not directly from this lane but surfaces on typecheck.

recommendations: Wrap get-desktop-custom-agents, set-desktop-custom-agents, delete-desktop-custom-agent, get-desktop-plugin-config in try/catch with console.error
Add ?.() to all window.api.setDesktopPluginConfig() calls in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx (toggleEnabled, removePlugin)
Refactor toggleEnabled and removePlugin to fetch fresh state via window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig?.() before computing updates (matching submitInstall pattern)
Fix writeQueues map type in ipc.ts to resolve TS2345 (Map<string, Promise<unknown>>)
